data.title: Google launches "AI Lab Assistant": can reason and analyze, propose hypotheses, and optimize solutions
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Google has launched an AI tool that can be used as a "virtual collaborator" for biomedical scientists. The new tool, tested by scientists from Stanford University and Imperial College London, can help scientists analyze large amounts of literature and propose new hypotheses with advanced reasoning capabilities. Google's artificial intelligence department DeepMind focuses on scientific research, and DeepMind's head, Demis Hassabis, also won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry with others last year for the technology developed by his team. Google said that in an experiment on liver fibrosis, all the methods proposed by this "assistant" showed the potential to inhibit the cause of the disease, and the activity was very positive. Google also pointed out that AI has the ability to optimize expert-generated solutions over time. "While this result still needs further verification, it provides a promising direction for using efficient AI systems to enhance and accelerate the work of expert scientists." (Jinshi)

data.title: Google Unveils AI Tool As Virtual Collaborator For Biomedical Scientists
data.description:
According to Odaily, Google has introduced an AI tool designed to serve as a 'virtual collaborator' for biomedical scientists. This innovative tool, tested by researchers from Stanford University and Imperial College London, leverages advanced reasoning capabilities to assist scientists in analyzing extensive literature and generating new hypotheses. DeepMind, Google's AI division, has prioritized scientific research, and its leader, Demis Hassabis, was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry last year for his team's technological advancements.

Google reported that during an experiment on liver fibrosis, all methods proposed by the AI 'assistant' demonstrated potential in inhibiting the disease's causes, showing highly positive activity. The company also highlighted the AI's ability to optimize expert-generated solutions over time. While these results require further validation, they present a promising direction for enhancing and accelerating the work of expert scientists using efficient AI systems.

data.title: Musk's Grok tops the list of free apps in the US AppStore
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Musk's AI company xAI's Grok APP has risen to the top of the US App Store free APP list, surpassing ChatGPT. (Jinshi)

data.title: Cryptocurrency App Adoption Surges In UAE
data.description: According to Foresight News, data from the analytics platform AppsFlyer indicates a significant rise in the adoption of cryptocurrency applications in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) in 2024. In 2023, the top 49 cryptocurrency apps in the UAE were downloaded 6.2 million times. This figure surged to 15 million downloads in 2024, marking a 41% increase. AppsFlyer noted that December experienced the highest spike in downloads, reaching 2.8 million.

data.title: Zaar Chain testnet based on Initia is now live
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, the Zaar Chain test network based on Initia is now online, and the first game of the ecosystem, Zaar Flip, has been launched. In the future, Zaar Flip will introduce more sections such as Zlinko, Zaar Raffles, Zaar Pot, and NFT Trading.
Zaar Chain is a game platform based on Interwoven Stack, providing users with a fair, transparent and seamless gaming experience. In addition, Zaar Chain will also integrate Sudoswap to consolidate its position as the cultural and gaming center of Initia.

data.title: Bitget has launched U-based AIOZ and GNO perpetual contracts
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to the official announcement, Bitget announced that it has launched U-based AIOZ and GNO perpetual contracts. Contract trading BOT will be opened simultaneously.

data.title: Nansen: 86% of LIBRA traders lost a total of $251 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Nansen data, the LIBRA scandal in Argentina last weekend caused investors to lose hundreds of millions of dollars in wealth. On-chain data tracked by Nansen showed that 86% of traders lost a total of $251 million, while the winners only made $180 million in profits. In other words, LIBRA is a "net negative wealth creation" event that may suck away market liquidity. Nansen data shows that between February 16 and 18, 70% of wallets that traded LIBRA ultimately suffered losses. (Coindesk)

data.description: According to Binance market data, the long/short ratio for BNB/USDT Perpetual Contracts is 1.9, with Long dominating. Currently, the funding rate is -0.0034%.﻿﻿
